human_verification.notes: Reviewed the home-services-and-field-consumer-services package for home-health field-quality specificity, CMS/eCFR/HHS source quality, service-support workflow fit, healthcare privacy and clinical-boundary discipline, payer prerequisite handling, adjacent-lane routing, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to home-health field-quality service support, evidence review, communication summaries, hold or release discipline, corrective-action packaging, and orchestrator return.

Authority sources cover CMS and eCFR HHA Conditions of Participation, CMS certification and survey context, Medicare home-health benefit and claims manuals, HH QRP, OASIS/iQIES, HHCAHPS, HHVBP, and HHS OCR minimum-necessary privacy guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope field quality, incomplete release evidence, repeat missed-visit defects, missing eligibility/referral/authorization prerequisites, PHI-sensitive requests, clinical judgment refusal, payer-policy refusal, unsupported policy exceptions, adjacent-lane handoff, source conflict, and tenant terminology adaptation.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/home-services-and-field-consumer-services/field-quality-home-services-specialist/ to preserve the queue-defined NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around clinical judgment, PHI disclosure, payer-policy interpretation, medical necessity, coding, utilization management, financial approval, policy exceptions, and final release authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated healthcare home-services field-quality overlay where final clinical, payer, privacy, coding, utilization-management, financial-approval, and release authority remain external.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
